summary: Task 087 is complete in substance. The repo now has a single server-only `SITE_URL` origin contract, homepage and public note metadata both consume the shared helper instead of request-context guessing, missing or invalid origin config fails closed by omitting canonicals and setting `robots` to noindex/nofollow, and the operator path is aligned across direct env docs, Compose docs, and `docker-compose.yml`. I did not find any overlap between canonical-origin ownership and owner-editable site branding.
satisfied_exit_criteria: One documented and implemented source of truth exists for the public canonical origin via `SITE_URL` in `src/features/public-site/config.ts`, with validation that rejects non-origin values and returns fail-closed `null` URL building for downstream consumers.

Homepage and note-page canonical generation rely on the shared metadata helper in `src/features/public-site/metadata.ts`, wired through `src/app/page.tsx` and `src/app/notes/[slug]/page.tsx`, with no request-header or runtime host guessing in those paths.

Missing-origin deployments fail closed for discovery at the implemented foundation layer: canonical metadata is omitted and `robots` is set to `index: false` and `follow: false`; invalid `SITE_URL` also fails closed.

Operator and env docs are aligned with the runtime contract: `.env.example`, `.env.compose.example`, `README.md`, `docker-compose.yml`, and the settings UI text all keep canonical origin as operator-owned deployment config rather than owner-editable branding.
The required checks supplied in the deterministic summary all passed: `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test:unit`, and `npm run verify`.
